message:
Major enhancement: Comprehensive testing infrastructure, CLI improvements, and agent automation

## Testing Infrastructure Improvements
- Added pytest summary reporting tool (scripts/report_pytest_summary.py) that parses JUnit XML reports and generates structured JSON summaries with test statistics, failure details, and exit codes
- Enhanced run_checks.sh with centralized PYTEST_TARGETS array for maintainability and added JUnit XML report generation with summary reporting
- Expanded test coverage across all modules with 2041 insertions, significantly improving test reliability
- Added comprehensive test fixtures and utilities for better test isolation and maintainability

## CLI and Resume Functionality Enhancements
- Improved mdwb_cli.py with advanced resume management capabilities:
  - Added list_pending_entries() and list_completed_entries() methods for better workflow visibility
  - Implemented sophisticated progress tracking with ETA calculations for long-running operations
  - Enhanced client context management with _client_ctx_or_shared() for connection reuse optimization
  - Added nullcontext support for cleaner context manager patterns
- Enhanced resume status command with detailed progress reporting, time estimates, and formatted output
- Improved error handling and recovery mechanisms for interrupted operations

## Agent Automation and Shared Utilities
- Significantly expanded scripts/agents/shared.py with robust utility functions:
  - Enhanced error handling with detailed exception tracking
  - Improved resource management for API calls and file operations
  - Added comprehensive validation and sanitization utilities
  - Implemented retry logic with exponential backoff for resilient operations
- Updated agent scripts (generate_todos.py, summarize_article.py) with improved shared functionality integration
- Added detailed documentation and usage examples in scripts/agents/README.md

## Store and Schema Enhancements
- Extended app/schemas.py with additional fields and validation rules for better data integrity
- Updated app/store.py with improved transaction handling and concurrent access management
- Added comprehensive manifest contract testing for API compatibility verification

## Test Coverage Expansions
- test_e2e_cli.py: Expanded from 121 to 363 lines with comprehensive end-to-end scenarios
- test_mdwb_cli_events.py: Added 262 new lines of event handling test coverage
- test_check_metrics.py: Enhanced with 172 additional lines for metrics validation
- test_store_manifest.py: Extended with 182 new lines covering manifest operations
- test_agent_scripts.py: Added 133 lines of agent functionality tests
- New test modules added:
  - test_report_pytest_summary.py: Tests for the new pytest summary reporter
  - test_pytest_cli_expansion.py: CLI argument expansion testing (placeholder for future implementation)
- Enhanced existing test modules with better assertions, edge case coverage, and fixture improvements

## Monitoring and Diagnostics
- Enhanced scripts/check_metrics.py with improved metric collection and reporting
- Updated scripts/show_latest_smoke.py with better smoke test result visualization
- Improved scripts/update_smoke_pointers.py for automated benchmark tracking
- Added production benchmark tracking files (benchmarks/production/latest.txt, latest_summary.md)

## Documentation and Operational Updates
- Updated README.md with latest features and usage instructions
- Enhanced docs/ops.md with operational best practices and troubleshooting guides
- Updated PLAN_TO_IMPLEMENT_MARKDOWN_WEB_BROWSER_PROJECT.md with implementation progress
- Added bead_status.txt for project milestone tracking
- Updated .beads/beads.left.jsonl with execution trace improvements

## Code Quality Improvements
- Consistent type hints and annotations throughout the codebase
- Improved error messages and logging for better debugging
- Enhanced resource cleanup and context manager usage
- Better separation of concerns and modular design patterns

This release represents a significant milestone in project maturity with robust testing, improved reliability, and enhanced developer experience. The comprehensive test coverage ensures stability while the CLI improvements provide better user experience for long-running operations.

+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import argparse
+import json
+from datetime import datetime, timezone
+from pathlib import Path
+from typing import Any
+import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
+
+
+def _iter_testsuites(root: ET.Element) -> list[ET.Element]:
+    if root.tag == "testsuite":
+        return [root]
+    if root.tag == "testsuites":
+        suites = list(root.findall("testsuite"))
+        if suites:
+            return suites
+    suites = list(root.findall(".//testsuite"))
+    return suites or [root]
+
+
+def summarize_junit(report_path: Path, *, exit_code: int) -> dict[str, Any]:
+    summary: dict[str, Any] = {
+        "report_path": str(report_path),
+        "generated_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
+        "exit_code": exit_code,
+        "status": "passed" if exit_code == 0 else "failed",
+        "tests": 0,
+        "failures": 0,
+        "errors": 0,
+        "skipped": 0,
+        "time": 0.0,
+        "failed_tests": [],
+    }
+    if not report_path.exists():
+        summary["warning"] = "JUnit report not found"
+        return summary
+
+    try:
+        root = ET.parse(report_path).getroot()
+    except ET.ParseError as exc:
+        summary["warning"] = f"Failed to parse JUnit XML: {exc}"
+        return summary
+
+    failed_tests: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
+    suites = _iter_testsuites(root)
+    for suite in suites:
+        summary["tests"] += int(suite.attrib.get("tests", 0) or 0)
+        summary["failures"] += int(suite.attrib.get("failures", 0) or 0)
+        summary["errors"] += int(suite.attrib.get("errors", 0) or 0)
+        summary["skipped"] += int(suite.attrib.get("skipped", 0) or 0)
+        try:
+            summary["time"] += float(suite.attrib.get("time", 0) or 0)
+        except (TypeError, ValueError):
+            pass
+        for case in suite.findall("testcase"):
+            failure_node = case.find("failure")
+            error_node = case.find("error")
+            problem_node = failure_node if failure_node is not None else error_node
+            if problem_node is None:
+                continue
+            message = problem_node.attrib.get("message") or (problem_node.text or "")
+            failed_tests.append(
+                {
+                    "name": case.attrib.get("name"),
+                    "classname": case.attrib.get("classname"),
+                    "type": problem_node.tag,
+                    "message": message.strip(),
+                }
+            )
+    summary["failed_tests"] = failed_tests
+    return summary
+
+
+def _write_summary(path: Path, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
+    path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+    path.write_text(json.dumps(payload, indent=2) + "\n", encoding="utf-8")
+
+
+def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
+    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Emit a JSON summary from a pytest JUnit XML report.")
+    parser.add_argument("--junit", required=True, help="Path to the JUnit XML file produced by pytest.")
+    parser.add_argument("--summary", required=True, help="Destination path for the JSON summary.")
+    parser.add_argument("--exit-code", required=True, type=int, help="Exit code returned by pytest.")
+    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
+
+    junit_path = Path(args.junit)
+    summary_path = Path(args.summary)
+    summary = summarize_junit(junit_path, exit_code=args.exit_code)
+    _write_summary(summary_path, summary)
+    status = summary["status"]
+    total = summary["tests"]
+    failures = summary["failures"] + summary["errors"]
+    print(f"Pytest summary ({status}): {failures} failures out of {total} tests — {summary_path}")
+    if summary.get("failed_tests"):
+        preview = ", ".join(filter(None, (case.get("name") for case in summary["failed_tests"][:5])))
+        print(f"Failing tests: {preview}")
+    return 0
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover - CLI entry
+    raise SystemExit(main())

+def _write_report(tmp_path: Path, content: str) -> Path:

+def test_summarize_junit_collects_failures(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
+    report = _write_report(
+        tmp_path,
+        """<testsuite tests="2" failures="1" errors="0" skipped="0" time="0.2">
+        <testcase classname="cli.test" name="test_ok" time="0.05"/>
+        <testcase classname="cli.test" name="test_fail" time="0.15">
+            <failure message="boom">Traceback</failure>
+        </testcase>
+        </testsuite>""",
+    )
+
+    summary = report_pytest_summary.summarize_junit(report, exit_code=1)
+
+    assert summary["tests"] == 2
+    assert summary["failures"] == 1
+    assert summary["status"] == "failed"
+    assert summary["failed_tests"] == [
+        {"name": "test_fail", "classname": "cli.test", "type": "failure", "message": "boom"}
+    ]

+
+    assert summary["warning"].startswith("JUnit report not found")
+    assert summary["status"] == "failed"
+    assert summary["tests"] == 0
+
+

+                <testcase classname="cli" name="test_ok" time="0.01"/>
+            </testsuite>
+        </testsuites>""",
+    )
+    summary_path = tmp_path / "summary.json"
+
+    exit_code = report_pytest_summary.main(
+        [
+            "--junit",
+            str(report),
+            "--summary",
+            str(summary_path),
+            "--exit-code",
+            "0",
+        ]
+    )
+
+    assert exit_code == 0
+    data = json.loads(summary_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
+    assert data["status"] == "passed"
+    assert data["tests"] == 1
